Updated: 5/7/2001
Important news in blue



Future DVD and VHS releases to be simultaneous.



Original Dragon Ball to air on Cartoon Network beginning June 25th. Certain voices will be chosen by fans via a poll.



Season 5 of Dragon Ball Z is currently in production. Air date set for Summer/Fall 2001.



Production to start soon on Yu Yu Hakusho and Blue Gender.



Movie #5 expected in October, #6 to follow shortly thereafter.



Infogrames will be the publisher of the upcoming DBZ game(s). Wizard Works has been chosen as the developer (ugh).



Note: The following dates are provided by the manufactures. Some retailers may receive their shipments early.

DVD

DBZ Frieza:
Summoning
 (5/8/2001)
Transformation
 (6/12/2001)
Revealed
 (July 2001)

DBZ Garlic Jr.:
Black Water Mist
 (June 2001)
Sacred Water
(July 2001)

DBZ Z Warriors Prepare
 (July 2001)

DBZ Androids:
Invincible
 (5/8/2001)

DBZ World Tournament:
Junior Division
 (6/12/2001)
The Draw
 (7/17/2001)
Blackout
 (7/17/2001)

VHS

DB Tournament:
Roshi's Request
(6/5/2001)
Turtle Hermit
(6/19/2001)

DBZ Perfect Cell:
Unstoppable
(6/19/2001)

DBZ Cell Games:
Ultimatum
(6/19/2001)

DBZ Great Saiya Man:
Crash Course
(5/15/2001)

DBZ World Tournament:
Junior Division
(5/15/2001)
The Draw
 (7/17/2001)
Blackout
 (7/17/2001)

DBZ Movie #4:
Lord Slug
(8/8/2001)

Manga

DB Part 3:
#13
(6/8/2001)

DBZ Part 4:
#6
(5/11/2001)
#7
(6/15/2001)

DB Graphic Novels:
Vol. 5
(5/18/2001)

DBZ Graphic Novels:
Vol. 5
(July 2001)

Latest Feature: T-Shirt Contest
Web Poll:
Have a piece of news? Submit it here!
Monday, 5/14/2001 @ 7:02 PM PDT by: Mr.E
We Show MTV how to Rock the Vote...
I was surprised at how long most of you guys have been supporters of Dragon Ball...we have a very loyal fanbase. And, out of sheer luck, the poll did not reset once. I am such a gleefull webmaster!

This next poll relates to the T-shirts again...what size tee suits you? I still haven't decided whether to offer each design in one size only or go with mutiple sizes. Having many sizes makes keeping shirts in stock very difficult. Anyhow, vote now, loyal DB fans!
Monday, 5/14/2001 @ 6:40 PM PDT by: Mr.E
Uncensored Re-Release of Graphic Novels...
Here's the news from Jason Thompson of Viz (courtesy of Julian):

I want you to know that I am putting together the uncensored DB v.1 right now to send to the printer next week. If all goes well, they will be available before the end of June. No additional press release fanfare or anything... we're just going to start sending them to stores & customers instead of the old censored ones, which will be out of print. After this I'll put the uncensored v.2 and 3 together. I know it's taken long enough, but it's definitely, finally happening!

This means you'll see uncensored versions of those graphic novels in a couple weeks. I'm guessing the 13 and up warning label will be included on the volumes like their current releases. This isn't exactly new news, but we now have a better idea of when we'll see these revised editions hit the shelves.

And on a completely unrelated note, Skull's section updates are being put on hold for a little longer...it seems someone dumped liquid on their keyboard and it is now non-functional. Remember kids, drinking and typing kills.
Monday, 5/14/2001 @ 4:14 PM PDT by: Mr.E
Z-Day Info...
In addition to DBZ airing twice a day now, Toonami is going to feature another "Z-Day":

Toonami Viewers Take Control on Z-Day

Viewers choice of Dragonball Z episodes air on Cartoon Network on May 25

On May 25, Toonami will air the top two DRAGONBALL Z episodes chosen by viewers. Fans will essentially program the first hour of the block by choosing their favorite DBZ episodes via online voting. The top two episodes, selected by the fans, will be featured 5-6 p.m. (ET, PT) during the Toonami afternoon action adventure block. From May 14 through May 21, viewers can log on to CartoonNetwork.com to cast their vote and then tune-in on May 25 to watch the top viewers‚ choice episodes.

Toonami, Cartoon Network's popular afternoon action-adventure programming block airs weekdays 5-7 p.m. (ET/PT) and features programs like Sailor Moon, Dragon Ball Z, Gundam Wing, Tenchi Muyo!, Outlaw Star and Ronin Warriors. Intermittently throughout the two-hour programming block, Toonami features popular music, computer generated 3-D animation segments and the block's host, TOM, an animated character who inhabits the space vessel Absolution.

Cartoon Network and Kid Rhino are teaming up to unleash an intergalactic blast of music from Toonami, the popular action-adventure franchise on Cartoon Network. TOONAMI: DEEP SPACE BASS will hit stores on Tuesday, May 15.

The TOONAMI: DEEP SPACE BASS CD will include a link that will give the fan access to an exclusive Web page. Once there, CD buyers can listen to an exclusive bonus track not included on the CD, as well as downloadable Toonami icons and wallpaper. Another feature, Toonami Movie Director, will allow Internet users to create their own music video using music and video images from Toonami.

I have a feeling Deep Space Bass is going to be really cool...Toonami has excellent music, definitely the best for any block of programming on TV. You can vote for your favorite DBZ episode right here. Thanks to Sam of the currently defunct SZR for the above...
Monday, 5/14/2001 @ 4:09 PM PDT by: Mr.E
Toonami Time Change Begins Today...
Sorry that I couldn't post this earlier so you guys on the East coast could get a heads up...living in CA has its downsides. Anyhow, the Toonami block has lost an hour beginning today...but DBZ is airing twice a day now until DB takes one of those slots. Here is the new schedule:

5:00 - Superman
5:30 - Tenchi Muyo
6:00 - Dragon Ball Z
6:30 - Dragon Ball Z

Don't worry, you haven't missed any new episodes. It's just a double dose of the same old medicine.
Friday, 5/11/2001 @ 1:22 PM PDT by: Mr.E
This Little Hummer Went to Indiana...
From the official site:

You can catch up to the Hummer this weekend in Indiana. The meeting place will be Wilshire Plaza in Mishawaka, IN from 12 pm to 4 pm. Be sure to make plans to visit the Hummer and the DBZ voice actors. Eric Johnson, voice of Trunks, and Dameon Clarke, voice of Cell, will be with the Hummer this weekend signing autographs and meeting fans. As usual you could win some cool stuff just for coming out. Next weekend the tour heads to the land of steel. The Hummer will make its weekend home in Pittsburgh at Ross Park Mall on Saturday from 12 pm to 4 pm. Start making plans now!

Stay plugged in here for continued updates on the coolest Hummer to ever hit
pavement.

More chances to meet the VAs...I swear, these voice actors leg around more than Riverdance.
Thursday, 5/10/2001 @ 10:04 PM PDT by: Mr.E
Something Important I Missed...
Thanks to WuFEi for catching this update to the official site's FAQ:

The Garlic Jr. DVD's that were scheduled to come out in June and July have been moved. We will let you know the rescheduled release dates as soon as they are available. Z Warriors Prepare has also been rescheduled. We are adding the Episodes 106-108 from the Trunks Prelude to Terror video to the Z Warriors Prepare DVD so it will contain 5 episodes (106-110). Continue to check here for updates on the VHS and DVD release schedule.

Now that is damn cool...even better than what I had suggested: lowering the price. Face it, 2 episodes for 25 bucks was a horrible deal...5 is a bargain. Looks like I'll be purchasing that DVD sometime in the future after-all.
Thursday, 5/10/2001 @ 3:10 PM PDT by: Mr.E
Release News Galore...
From the official site:

This just in: World Tournament - The Draw and World Tournament - Blackout, which are scheduled for VHS release on July 17, are anticipated to be the first videos to have simultaneous release of VHS and DVD. That means you will be able to get these videos on DVD July 17. This is the anticipated schedule, which means that it is not final and there could be a change. This is very exciting news. In addition, it is anticipated that every release after these will be simultaneous with VHS and DVD, providing that any new video release has not aired on Cartoon Network. Be sure to regularly check the FAQ's for updates on the VHS and DVD release schedules.

I had made a blunder a couple days ago...I put the two VHS World Tournament release dates under DVD releases by mistake. Looks like my error actually coincides with fact. With that being said, the releases on the left side of the page have been updated once again.

BTW, anyone know where I can find a list of Viz release dates? For some reason, ANS stopped posting them...

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragon Ball, nor did we have any part in the production process. There are some items on this website that may be questionable for those under 13 years of age. Dragon Ball was intended for young children in Japan who don't view such material as offensive.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragon Ball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."

-- Lance
